differential_diagnoses: Sickle cell–related cerebral vaso-occlusive crisis (silent cerebral infarction/ischemia)
next_steps: Admission to the intensive care unit for neurologic monitoring
Strict blood pressure control to address potential hypertensive etiology of PRES
Initiation of anti-seizure medication such as levetiracetam
Urgent hematology consultation for consideration of red cell exchange transfusion
Lumbar puncture to exclude central nervous system infection or occult hemorrhage
Ophthalmologic evaluation to assess for signs of increased intracranial pressure

differential_diagnoses: Metabolic encephalopathy (e.g., hypomagnesemia or electrolyte derangement)
Drug-induced seizures (e.g., oxycodone or gabapentin interaction)
Structural intracranial pathology (e.g., remote microhemorrhage or sequelae of previous vasculopathy)
next_steps: Monitor in the intensive care unit with continuous EEG for subclinical seizure activity
Obtain serum electrolytes including magnesium, calcium, and phosphorus
Initiate or optimize anti-seizure medication (e.g., levetiracetam)
Consult hematology for assessment of transfusion thresholds and need for urgent exchange transfusion
Manage blood pressure strictly but cautiously to prevent ischemic injury from over-correction
Perform a detailed medication review for potential triggers of lowered seizure threshold
Neuro-ophthalmology consultation to evaluate for signs of increased intracranial pressure or retinal changes
